behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Rejected Unit because the behavior explicitly targets the module boundary: "request sent to the external API" and "complete list" can fail in request-builder/client wiring even if helper functions pass. Rejected higher levels because no real OS/subprocess/network behavior is required and correctness does not depend on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a stub API client that captures outbound payloads; invoke classification with a known taxonomy and assert every valid label is present in the dispatched request.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Rejected Unit because failure can occur at response parsing/validation handoff across modules, and behavior is phrased as "When the API returns... the module returns". Rejected Agentic/System since a deterministic stubbed API response is sufficient; no real model quality or OS/network effects are needed.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API response returning an out-of-taxonomy label; run through the module entrypoint and assert the caller receives "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Unit. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because behavior is explicitly about one local transformation: "normalization step" with "identical raw API responses"; no module boundary or I/O is required to expose nondeterministic normalization bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization directly with the same raw response fixture multiple times and assert identical final label output each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Agentic. Runner-up: System. Rejected System because the behavior depends on real model decision stability, not just transport: "repeated live API calls" and label consistency are about AI output, which mocks hide. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real provider calls with identical document+taxonomy over a fixed window; record provider responses and final labels; assert label consistency when provider response content is unchanged, and only allow drift when response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Rejected Unit because behavior includes orchestration at dispatch boundary: "before dispatch" and policy application can fail when config, tokenizer, and request-construction modules interact. Rejected System/Agentic because this can be validated with in-process wiring and no real model output.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget, pass an over-budget document through the module, and assert the pre-dispatch policy outcome (truncate or reject) and that dispatched payload/token estimate respects configured limits.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Agentic. Runner-up: System. Rejected System because correctness depends on real AI API behavior/metadata: "Provider-reported token usage" from real calls can diverge from local estimates, and stubs would mask that. Rejected Workflow because only one model call is required, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents against the provider; collect provider-reported token usage and assert each call is within configured budget threshold.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Integration. Runner-up: System. Rejected System because a deterministic stub can reproduce "API signals rate too high" and verify retry/wait control flow without real network. Rejected Agentic because model output quality is irrelevant; this is retry orchestration logic.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API client that returns a rate-limit error then success; assert the module performs wait/backoff and retry, and ultimately returns success to caller instead of immediate error.
